Title: Concurrent activation of OsAMT1;2 and OsGOGAT1 leads to enhanced nitrogen use efficiency under nitrogen limitation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glutamine and 2-oxoglutarate into glutamate using NADH in the GS/GOGAT cycle
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and drives assimilation/remobilization toward grain nitrogen and protein accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a bottleneck/key control step of the GS/GOGAT cycle in the paper discussion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves grain nutritional quality and supports plant growth under nitrogen limitation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Grain; flag leaf remobilization stream; newly emerging leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Important for primary ammonium assimilation in roots and reutilization/remobilization of nitrogen in developing organs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enhanced in activation-tagging seedlings and flag leaves; associated with improved N remobilization at vegetative and reproductive stages
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen limitation; paddy field cultivation; outdoor low-N soil; 15N labeling/chase assays; hydroponics with ammonium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of OsGOGAT1 enhances tolerance to nitrogen limitation, increases ammonium uptake indirectly, improves nitrogen remobilization, and raises grain nitrogen, protein, and amino acid content; when combined with OsAMT1;2 it improves yield under low N
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Activation tagging; qRT-PCR; 15N uptake assay; 15N remobilization tracing; GS activity assay; metabolite/nitrogen/protein/amino acid quantification; field and low-N pot trials
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Activation-tagged OsGOGAT1 lines showed enhanced expression and improved seedling tolerance to nitrogen limitation. 2. OsGOGAT1 activation increased nitrogen remobilization from old to newly emerging leaves and from flag leaves during grain filling. 3. OsGOGAT1 activation increased grain nitrogen, protein, and amino acid content, and in combination with OsAMT1;2 improved grain yield under low nitrogen.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong target for improving NUE and grain nutritional quality; best deployed together with OsAMT1;2 to balance ammonium uptake and assimilation and avoid the yield penalties seen in single-gene activation lines.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single OsGOGAT1 activation lines showed reduced plant height, lower spikelet number/fertility, reduced seed size/weight, and lower productivity in paddy fields despite higher grain N/protein.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes that OsGOGAT1-mediated improvement is especially evident for nitrogen remobilization; single-gene activation improved grain quality but not field productivity under prolonged low N.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates high-affinity uptake/transport of ammonium into root cells
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream assimilation into amino acids and grain nitrogen/protein
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key uptake step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves grain nutritional quality and supports growth under nitrogen limitation when uptake is coupled to assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots for ammonium uptake; grain for final nitrogen/protein enrichment
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cell surface/plasma membrane of root tip cells and central cylinder (as described for expression context)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-specific; expressed in the central cylinder and cell surface of root tips; ammonium-inducible
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enhanced in activation-tagging lines while maintaining root-specific pattern
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen limitation; hydroponics with ammonium; 15N-labeled ammonium uptake assays; methylammonium treatment; paddy field and outdoor low-N conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of OsAMT1;2 increases ammonium uptake and seedling tolerance to nitrogen limitation, raises grain N/protein/amino acids, and synergizes with OsGOGAT1 to improve NUE and grain yield under low N
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Activation tagging; qRT-PCR; 15N uptake assay; methylammonium sensitivity assay; nitrogen/protein/amino acid quantification; field and low-N pot trials
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Activation-tagged OsAMT1;2 lines showed increased root expression and higher ammonium uptake capacity. 2. OsAMT1;2 activation improved seedling performance under low nitrogen and increased grain nitrogen, protein, and amino acid levels. 3. Combining OsAMT1;2 activation with OsGOGAT1 activation produced superior NUE and low-N grain yield compared with either single activation line.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering ammonium uptake, but should be stacked with assimilation genes such as OsGOGAT1 to avoid metabolic imbalance and yield penalties under field conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single OsAMT1;2 activation lines had lower field biomass, reduced spikelet number and fertility, smaller grains/seed weight, earlier leaf senescence, and no yield improvement under prolonged low N despite improved grain N content.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study chose activation tagging to enhance endogenous expression while retaining native organ specificity; OsAMT1;2 alone did not improve leaf-to-leaf N remobilization because expression remained root-restricted.
